constraints.description: Activities are expected to start at TRL 2 and achieve TRL 5 by the end of the project.
All proposals are expected to allocate tasks for cohesion activities with the co-programmed European Partnership on AI, data, and robotics (ADRA).
All proposals are expected to allocate tasks for cohesion activities with the CSA HORIZON-CL4-2025-03-HUMAN-18: GenAI4EU central Hub.
Communicable results should be shared with the European R&D community through the AI-on-demand platform.
Projects selected in this topic should link to the resources offered by the AI Factories, including the Data Labs.
All proposals are expected to incorporate mechanisms for assessing and demonstrating progress, including qualitative and quantitative KPIs, benchmarking, and progress monitoring.
When possible, proposals should build on and reuse public results from relevant previous funded actions.
Maximum EU contribution per project is €19,000,000.
Financial support to third parties (FSTP) is permitted: maximum €300,000 per grant (stage 2), €2,250,000 (stage 3), and €2,550,000 per third party.

To ensure portfolio coverage, grants will be awarded to applications not only in order of ranking, but also to at least one proposal focusing on each of three designated sectors (healthcare, advanced manufacturing, in-vehicle autonomous driving), subject to proposals passing all evaluation thresholds.

Participation is limited to legal entities established in EU Member States, Iceland, Norway, and associated countries: Canada, Israel, Republic of Korea, New Zealand, Switzerland, and the United Kingdom.
Entities controlled by non-eligible countries may be excluded unless guarantees are provided.
Results may be validated in the Testing and Experiment Facilities and further deployed via the European Digital Innovation Hubs (EDIHs).
Projects will contribute to the Apply AI strategy.
Proposals should build on or seek collaboration with relevant projects and develop synergies with other relevant International, European, national, or regional initiatives.
The submission procedure is single-stage with a deadline of 2026-04-15.
Maximum project duration is 48 months. Note: not explicitly stated in topic text; 48 months is the standard maximum for large RIA actions under Horizon Europe. Verify against General Annex B.
constraints.assumption_note: The 48-month maximum project duration is not explicitly stated in the topic text of the call extract. The value is taken from the selected_call.json binding note which flags it as a standard maximum for RIA actions pending verification against General Annex B. The topic text does not include a duration field in either source file.
